title: Flaky Moroccan Eggplant Tagine
description: A comforting, low-sodium Moroccan tagine featuring tender eggplant braised in a fragrant tomato and spice sauce, finished with fresh herbs and a touch of honey.
instructions: Heat 1 Tbsp olive oil in a large oven‑proof skillet over medium‑high heat. Add the eggplant rounds, cooking until lightly browned on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate and set aside. In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 Tbsp olive oil.
Sauté the chopped onion in the oil until translucent, about 4 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, ginger, and cayenne pepper, cooking for 30 seconds to toast the spices.
Add the canned tomatoes with their juice, vegetable broth, honey, and smoked paprika, stirring to combine. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Return the browned eggplant slices to the skillet, nestling them into the sauce. Cover with a lid or foil and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the eggplant is tender and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Remove the skillet from the oven and stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning—add a pinch of salt only if needed to enhance flavor.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley and cilantro over the tagine just before serving.
Serve hot, accompanied by couscous, flatbread, or a side of steamed greens for a complete meal.
